title: pseudalbizzia decandra
intro: Pseudalbizzia decandra, also known as Chittagong fever, is a plant species native to India and Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues. The root and stem of Pseudalbizzia decandra are used in herbal preparations, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties, providing relief from pain, inflammation, and fever. The plant is often prepared as a decoction or powder for medicinal use, although its efficacy and safety are still being researched and studied further.
